inputs.label: Total Work Content (TWC)
inputs.businessContext: Sum of all standard times for operations in the sewing line (ISO 9001 time measurement).

businessContext_i18n.en: Sum of all standard times for operations in the sewing line (ISO 9001 time measurement).

inputs.label: Number of Operators
inputs.businessContext: Total direct labor assigned to the line.

businessContext_i18n.en: Total direct labor assigned to the line.

inputs.label: Takt Time
inputs.businessContext: Customer demand rate: available production time divided by required output (Lean manufacturing).

businessContext_i18n.en: Customer demand rate: available production time divided by required output (Lean manufacturing).

inputs.label: Bottleneck Station Time
inputs.businessContext: Longest operation time among all stations (Theory of Constraints).

businessContext_i18n.en: Longest operation time among all stations (Theory of Constraints).

inputs.label: Line Balance Type
inputs.businessContext: Configuration of the sewing line: straight, U-shape, or modular (Lean layout principles).

businessContext_i18n.en: Configuration of the sewing line: straight, U-shape, or modular (Lean layout principles).

inputs.label: Allow Rebalancing
inputs.businessContext: If true, the tool will suggest operator reassignment to improve balance.

businessContext_i18n.en: If true, the tool will suggest operator reassignment to improve balance.

rules.message: Total Work Content must be greater than zero.
Number of Operators must be at least 1.
Bottleneck time must not exceed takt time to meet demand.
Average work per operator exceeds takt time; line cannot meet demand.

thresholds.message: Balance efficiency below 80% – significant idle time or overload.
Balance efficiency below 60% – line is severely unbalanced.
Bottleneck exceeds takt time – demand cannot be met without overtime or rebalancing.

formulas.name: Theoretical Minimum Operators
formulas.description: Minimum number of operators needed to meet takt time (Lean ideal).

formulas.name: Balance Efficiency
formulas.description: Percentage of time the line is effectively utilized (Six Sigma metric).

formulas.name: Idle Time per Cycle
formulas.description: Total idle time across all operators per cycle (waste identification).

formulas.name: Line Capacity
formulas.description: Maximum output per hour based on bottleneck (Theory of Constraints).

formulas.name: Demand Fulfillment Rate
formulas.description: Percentage of demand that can be met with current bottleneck (WERC logistics metric).

formulas.name: Labor Productivity
formulas.description: Output per operator per hour (ISO 22400 productivity KPI).

formulas.name: Overall Line Balance Score
formulas.description: Composite index combining efficiency, demand fulfillment, and waste reduction (0-100 scale).

primary.label: Overall Line Balance Score
primary.description: Composite performance index (0-100) for the sewing line.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Excess Motion Waste
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Operators walking or reaching beyond standard work area (Lean 7 wastes).
hiddenLossDrivers.impact: Increases bottleneck time by up to 15%.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Quality Defects Rework
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Time spent reworking defective garments (Six Sigma DPMO).
hiddenLossDrivers.impact: Reduces effective total work content by 5-20%.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Machine Downtime
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Unplanned stops due to maintenance or setup (OEE metric).

suggestedActions.label: Rebalance Operators
suggestedActions.description: Reassign work content to reduce bottleneck time to ≤ takt time.

suggestedActions.label: Reduce Bottleneck Time
suggestedActions.description: Apply Kaizen to the bottleneck station (method improvement, tooling, or automation).

suggestedActions.label: Cross-Train Operators
suggestedActions.description: Train operators on multiple stations to enable flexible rebalancing.

suggestedActions.label: Implement Andon System
suggestedActions.description: Real-time visual alerts for line stoppages to reduce downtime.
